channel.title: Ageless Linux: Updates
channel.description: News and Commentary from the Ageless Linux project

item.title: Fax Your Rep — Ageless Linux
item.description: Send a one-page fax to your California legislator about AB 1043. No age verification required.

item.title: The Shame Wall — Legislators Without a Fax Number — Ageless Linux
item.description: Public fax coverage for every target state. Ten U.S. senators and representatives have a DC fax. Everyone else has decided that faxes are beneath them.

item.title: Fax Your Rep — CYOA web form and transparency page shipped
item.description: New /fax.html: address entry, legislator lookup, four CYOA persona paths (parent, developer, student, privacy advocate), section-cited fax template, live preview, send flow, and sharing. Every step teaches one thing about AB 1043. The form is the argument. Paired with /fax-transparency.html — "The Shame Wall" — which documents fax coverage across Congress and target state legislatures: 10 of 538 federal members keep a DC fax line; 120 of 120 California state legislators are reachable via the algorithmic Capitol fax pattern. Tagline: "No age verification required to send a fax."

item.title: Michigan pulls Digital Age Assurance Act — first withdrawal of model legislation
item.description: Michigan HB 4429 and SB 284 — identical bipartisan bills based on Digital Childhood Alliance model legislation — pulled by sponsors April 6, 2026 after privacy advocates identified critical gaps: no limits on data use, no restrictions on combining data with other personal information, no data deletion requirements. First state to withdraw bills based on this model. State law tracker updated with new "Withdrawn" section and timeline entry.

item.title: MidnightBSD ships first base-system age verification daemon
item.description: MidnightBSD merged PR #302: a complete age verification stack in the base OS. The aged daemon stores per-user age/DOB in SQLite, exposed over a Unix socket, enabled by default. Includes agectl CLI, agev(3) library API in libutil, and age-based Unix groups (age4p, age13p, age16p, age18p) for mport package gating. Purely self-declared — no external verification. PR body acknowledges it "will not comply with Brazil or the proposed law in New York." First operating system to ship a base-system age verification daemon. Geographic COPYRIGHT exclusion of CA and Brazil remains alongside.

item.title: EFF resource hub added to citations; exclusion statistics documented
item.description: Added the EFF's comprehensive age verification resource hub (eff.org/Age) to citations. New citation cards for the hub overview and "Who Is Harmed" exclusion statistics (15M without licenses, 43% of trans Americans lack correct ID, 100M with facial differences). Two new Further Reading entries for the hub and "Does the Tech Even Work" technical analysis.

item.title: systemd ships birthDate field; distro tracker updated
item.description: systemd PR #40954 merged March 18, adding a birthDate field to user records — the data layer for the emerging age verification stack. Distro tracker updated with systemd (upstream), NixOS, Artix Linux, and Kicksecure/Whonix status change. Emerging Standard section rewritten for the systemd + xdg-desktop-portal pipeline.

item.title: Ageless Linux — State Law Tracker
item.description: Added Illinois, New York, and Brazil to state law tracker; expanded timeline and penalty tables

item.title: Ageless Linux — Distro Compliance Tracker
item.description: Added Arch Linux 32 (excluding CA + BR), updated MidnightBSD to reflect Brazil exclusion, added source citations
